idea-plugin.name: Flowable Keys
idea-plugin.description:
Stop guessing Flowable model keys — and stop copy-pasting them from Design.

Flowable Keys makes the model keys in your Java code first-class, IDE-aware references — resolved against the models that actually live in your project. It indexes every Flowable model you keep in the repo: the artifacts you exported from Design (the app .zip / deployment -bar, holding .bpmn/.cmmn/.dmn/.form/.data/.service/…) or the Flowable Design *-models workspace — then completes the matching key at every Flowable API call site, searchable by key, by name, or by any fragment (type 0061 to get KYC-DO-0061).

What you get

  • Context-aware key completion at every Flowable API position that takes a model key — process, case, decision, form, event, channel, data object, master data, service, action, agent, knowledge base, template, security policy, query, variable extractor, sequence, SLA, dashboard component, data dictionary, page and unified work definitions. Both org.flowable.* and com.flowable.*.
  • Cascade completion for fluent chains — operation("…") completes the operations of the data object / service resolved from the sibling definitionKey(…) / serviceKey(…) (constant references are resolved), and value("…", …) completes that operation's input fields. DMN variable("…", …) offers the decision's input/output variables.
  • More than keys — completion for message & signal names, process variables, task-definition keys and activity IDs. Task/activity completion is scoped to the processDefinitionKey / caseDefinitionKey in the same query chain when present, so you see only that model's ids.
  • Model → model references in BPMN/CMMN XML — the keys that live inside the models themselves (callActivity calledElement, flowable:formKey, decisionRef, CMMN caseRef / processRef) get the same completion, Ctrl/Cmd-click navigation and broken-key inspection as Java call sites — so a calledElement pointing at a missing process is caught before deployment.
  • Broken-key inspection — an unknown key (in Java or model XML) is flagged inline with a "did you mean …?" quick-fix to the closest real key.
  • Generate a typed data-object bean — Alt/Option-Enter on a data-object definitionKey("…") writes a Java POJO from the model's field mappings, so query results map onto a bean instead of the generic container.
  • Liquibase aware — inside a changelog it completes <column> names and tableName from the backing service model, and flags columns that no backing Flowable model defines.
  • Go to definition & Find Usages — Ctrl/Cmd-click a key literal to jump straight to the model file; hover for its type, name and location. Find Usages on a delegate class or an ${bean.method()} expression lists the model files that reference it.
  • Generate model constants — one action writes a typed Java class (FlowableModelKeys.Process.ORDER_FULFILMENT, model name in Javadoc) and keeps it in sync automatically as models are added, renamed or removed.

How it feels

  • runtimeService.startProcessInstanceByKey("<caret>") → your process keys.
  • cmmnRuntimeService.createCaseInstanceBuilder().caseDefinitionKey("<caret>") → your case keys.
  • dataObjectRuntimeService.createDataObjectInstanceQuery().definitionKey(KEY).operation("<caret>") → that data object's operations; .value("<caret>", …) → the operation's fields.
  • Press completion twice to list all model keys of every type, regardless of the call site.

Fires inside string literals and at bare arguments alike (inserting a quoted key), with Flowable keys ranked to the top. Zero configuration — open a project that contains your exported Flowable models and start typing.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.2.0

  • Infix key search — completion now matches any fragment of a key, so typing 0061 at definitionKey("…") proposes KYC-DO-0061 (start / word-boundary matches still rank first).
  • Scoped task/activity completion — taskDefinitionKey / activityId are narrowed to the sibling processDefinitionKey / caseDefinitionKey in the same query chain, falling back to the project-wide union otherwise.
  • Model → model references in BPMN/CMMN XML — completion, Ctrl/Cmd-click navigation and a broken-key inspection (with quick fix) for calledElement, flowable:formKey, decisionRef, decisionTableReferenceKey, CMMN caseRef / processRef.
  • Generate a typed data-object bean — Alt/Option-Enter on a data-object definitionKey("…") writes a Java POJO from the model's field mappings.
  • Long project scans now honour cancellation (no UI stall while indexing / finding usages).

0.1.0

  • Initial release: context-aware Flowable model-key completion, cascade completion, broken-key inspection, key navigation & Find Usages, model-constants generation, and Liquibase changelog awareness.

localInspection.displayName: Liquibase column not defined in Flowable model

action.description: Generate a Java class with all Flowable model keys as constants (kept in sync)
Scan the project and list all indexed Flowable model keys
